response.justification: The candidate is a seasoned generalist with strong web/full-stack and some systems-level experience, but their C++ exposure is limited to a brief stint at National Instruments (2014–2015) and a bachelor thesis project. The role demands deep, current C++/STL expertise, message-broker architectures, MQTT/IoT protocols, and IEC 62443 secure-development practices in industrial automation — none of which are evidenced. Their high-performance DLang work shows relevant concurrency and low-latency skills, but the core stack mismatch is too large for a senior C++ position.
strengths: Extensive overall software experience (~15+ years) across web, embedded, and desktop
Low-latency, high-throughput systems work (async I/O, fibers, caching) at Dunnhumby
Strong engineering culture: TDD, CI/CD, mentoring, workshops, and open-source contributions
concerns: Minimal and outdated professional C++ experience (roughly 6 months, a decade ago)
No industrial automation, IoT protocol (MQTT, Modbus, OPC UA), or IEC 62443 exposure
Recent focus on TypeScript/Node full-stack work diverges from the role's systems focus
